26. No complaints were received during the reporting years. The Company is taking proactive steps to implement a comprehensive tracking system and establish a robust mechanism for monitoring and addressing grievances going forward.
27. Material issues identified are referred from the Sustainability Accounting Standards Board (SASB) 2023-24 version. SASB Standards are maintained and enhanced by the International Sustainability Standards Board (ISSB). This follows the SASB's merger with the International Integrated Reporting Council (IIRC) into the Value Reporting Foundation (VRF) and subsequent consolidation into the IFRS® Foundation in 2022. The latest standards have been accessed at https://sasb.ifrs.org/ on 24nd April, 2025 at 14:59 IST.
All the Financial Implication from risks are Negative. There has been no negative impact in the reporting period of 2024-2025.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfBusinessActivity: The Company’s primary focus is on the manufacturing of advanced intermediates and specialty chemicals, which require specialized chemistry and cutting-edge technology.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: The Company, operating in the B2B sector, serves a diverse client base across the chemical industry, including multinational, regional, and domestic companies in pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, materials science, oil & gas, and textiles. It’s product portfolio is strategically designed to meet the specialized requirements of various segments, such as coatings, high-performance photography, additives, and oil & gas, among others.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForGrievanceRedressPoli: The Company interacts with various communities on need to need basis through its CSR activities. The Company also has an internal mechanism in place.

SEBI prescribed mechanism of SCORES is in place and shareholders can register their grievances at https://scores.gov.in.
The complaints of the shareholders are resolved by RTA and the Company as per the mechanism prescribed by SEBI.

SEBI prescribed mechanism of SCORES is in place and shareholders can register their grievances at https://scores.gov.in.
The complaints of the shareholders are resolved by RTA and the Company as per the mechanism prescribed by SEBI.

The Company has implemented a comprehensive procedure to address the grievances of its employees. If an employee has a grievance, they can reach out to their immediate manager as the first point of contact. If the issue remains unresolved, the respective business.
HR will be the next point of contact, responsible for investigating the matter and providing a resolution based on fair and transparent dealings.

The Company has implemented a comprehensive escalation process for customer complaints, which is also outlined on the Company’s website for easy access.
Clients can follow a three-step procedure to address their concerns: 1. Customers can submit their grievances through various channels such as email, phone number, or complaint letters. 2. If dissatisfied with the initial resolution, customers have the option to directly email the Deputy Vice President of Customer Support. 3. If still unsatisfied, customers can escalate their concern by sending an email directly to the head of customer support.

Yes, the Company actively engages with its value chain partners through E-mail, , site visit, group meetings , one on one interactions telephonic communication, SMS, exhibitions gatherings, etc. to understand their needs and resolve their grievances if any.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Innovation and digitalization serve as key drivers of the Company’s efforts to streamline operations and stay responsive to shifting consumer demands. Embracing emerging technologies, the Company actively seeks to expand its reach by tapping into under-served markets and developing forward-looking solutions. In parallel, it maintains a strong commitment to ethical conduct—ensuring full regulatory compliance, honest and responsible marketing, and clear, accurate labeling of products and services. By promoting fairness and transparency across all customer interactions, the Company takes deliberate steps to avoid discriminatory or exploitative practices, reinforcing trust and addressing broader societal expectations around integrity in business communication.

Tailored pricing models that reflect the economic and healthcare realities of different regions can serve as powerful enablers of innovation, market expansion, and collaborative growth. By aligning pricing with global needs, the Company positions itself to unlock new opportunities while delivering value to shareholders. With a diversified presence across sectors—including pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, material sciences, coatings, advanced photography, additives, and oil & gas the Company ensures its solutions remain accessible and impactful across a wide spectrum of industries.

The use of fossil fuels in manufacturing and cogeneration activities contributes to the Company’s greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions footprint. As regulatory frameworks around climate change continue to strengthen, the Company may encounter rising operational costs and compliance obligations. These evolving environmental standards present both a risk to business continuity and a call to action for more sustainable production practices.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: In its pursuit of environmental responsibility, the Company is steadily increasing sustainability into its operations. Efforts to lower energy consumption span across functions- ranging from the adoption of energy-efficient infrastructure and smart workstations to promoting virtual collaboration through videoconferencing. Complementing these measures are targeted awareness strategies that encourage energy-conscious behaviour among employees. Regular energy audits further support this agenda by identifying actionable opportunities for conservation and driving continuous improvement in resource efficiency.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Product safety remains a critical priority, as incidents involving personal harm, manufacturing flaws, or insufficient risk disclosures can trigger substantial liability claims. Such events often carry financial consequences, including costly recalls and reputational damage. For the company, taking a proactive stance through rigorous quality control, transparent communication, and compliance with safety standards serves as a strategic defense. These practices not only reduce the likelihood of regulatory action and recalls but also help safeguard investor confidence and preserve long-term market trust.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Ensuring product safety begins with comprehensive testing protocols and quality assurance systems designed to identify and eliminate potential health or safety hazards. The Company places strong emphasis on controlling the composition of its products - carefully monitoring chemicals, materials, and ingredients to uphold the highest safety benchmarks. This vigilant approach to oversight is applied consistently across all industries the Company operates in, reinforcing trust and regulatory compliance at every stage of the product lifecycle.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: In a fiercely competitive talent market - where success hinges on expertise in areas such as product innovation, clinical research, regulatory strategy, and market execution- the Company recognizes that its people are its greatest asset. To stay ahead, it is building a workforce that thrives on diversity, inclusion, and continuous learning.

The Company address ESG-related risks and responsibilities throughout their supply chains, spanning environmental impact, ethical sourcing, labour rights, and human welfare. The Company embraces its role in driving sustainable value beyond its operations by promoting responsible conduct across its supplier ecosystem. Through a structured approach to supplier management- including rigorous evaluation, ongoing monitoring, and collaborative engagement- the Company actively works to identify and reduce environmental and social risks.

The Company’s approach to community impact centres on meaningful engagement, environmental equity, and economic inclusion. By supporting local employment, collaborating with nearby businesses, the Company ensures that its operations are both responsible and responsive to community needs. This strategy goes beyond compliance, it reflects a commitment to shared value.

Customer welfare sits at the heart of long-term business performance, shaping how products are conceived, delivered, and supported. The Company focuses on developing solutions that not only meet evolving consumer demands but also reflect broader societal expectations around fairness, safety, and accountability. Evaluating how the Company manages product design, quality assurance, and delivery standards provides insight into its ability to safeguard customer interests and uphold lasting relationships in a competitive marketplace.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: To safeguard customer welfare, the Company takes a forward-looking approach to risk management - prioritizing speed, transparency, and accountability. When concerns arise, immediate corrective measures are put in place, backed by reinforced safety protocols and open communication with stakeholders. Beyond such steps, the Company invests in ongoing risk evaluation and leverages customer insights to inform continuous improvement.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Upholding business ethics is a vital responsibility that ensures company practices comply not only with local laws but also with broader industry standards and global norms, protecting the interests of all stakeholders. It also requires attentiveness to the dynamic nature of ethical expectations, which can vary across different jurisdictions and cultural environments. Maintaining high ethical standards means delivering professional and responsible services consistently, while actively avoiding conflicts of interest, misrepresentation, biasness, or negligence. Ultimately, strong ethical conduct forms the foundation of trust and integrity essential for sustainable business success.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: The Company has developed a governance framework built around a comprehensive suite of policies and procedures for ethics business practice. These guidelines offer clear direction to employees and stakeholders at every level of the value chain, reinforcing the company’s unwavering commitment to ethics, transparency, and sustainability. This commitment is actively supported through thorough employee training programs and the enforcement of rigorous policies and procedures designed to guarantee fairness and precision in service delivery.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PerformanceOfTheEntityAgainstT:
As a Company, we recognize the importance of environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors in building a sustainable and ethical business. We are aware of the potential impact of our actions on the world and remain committed to driving positive change.
Our ESG journey began with defining our core values and aligning them with ESG principles. This was followed by a materiality assessment to identify the most relevant ESG issues for our company and stakeholders.
Throughout this process, we actively engaged with all relevant stakeholders to ensure that our strategy aligns with the needs and expectations of our employees, customers, investors, and communities.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible:
Our commitment to sustainable, long-term growth is deeply rooted in environmental, social, and governance (ESG) principles. Recognizing the growing importance of ESG in today’s business landscape, we are dedicated to integrating these principles into our core operations. Beyond regulatory compliance, our approach is driven by a genuine belief in the value ESG brings to both our company and society.
To uphold this commitment, we have implemented proactive measures to minimize our environmental footprint and promote social responsibility. Our initiatives include adopting renewable energy sources, implementing waste reduction strategies, and actively managing carbon emissions. Moreover, we are fostering sustainable practices across our supply chain, ensuring that our impact extends beyond our direct operations.
By embedding ESG principles into our business strategy, we are enhancing resilience, driving sustainability, and contributing to the broader goal of creating a more sustainable future for generations to come.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheHighestAuthorityRe: Dr. Aman Ashvin Desai (Whole-time Director) DIN - 00043633 Phone Number- 0261-6603360 Email Id-compliance@aether.co.in
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT: The Board Committee acts as the governing body responsible for overseeing the integration of sustainability principles into the company’s strategic plans and daily operations. Comprising Board members, it plays a key role in decision-making on sustainability-related matters, ensuring that sustainability objectives align with the company’s mission and values. The committee monitors progress toward these goals, provides guidance on sustainability initiatives, and ensures compliance with relevant standards. Oversight on such matters is crucial in driving sustainability efforts, maintaining transparency, and upholding accountability across the organization.

Some of the Policies on the Company’s Intranet include - the Anti bribery Policy, Ethical Business practice Policy (P1), Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH) Policy (P5), Quality, Environment, Health, and Safety Policy (P2, P6, P9), Information and Cyber Security (ICS) Policy (P9) and Supplier Code of Conduct (P1,P2). The provisions of Conflict of interest, Gift Policy (exchanging and giving), Freedom of Association, Anti- bribery and Anti-corruption are covered under the Code of Conduct and other SOPs of the Company.
Essential 3- Yes, certain company policies are extended to our value chain partners to ensure alignment with our standards such as - Prevention of Sexual Harassment Policy and Quality, Environment, Health, and Safety Policy, Ethical Business Practice Policy and Supplier Code of Conduct .
Essential 10 -
Performance against above policies and follow up action -
The Company’s management conducts thorough reviews of all relevant policies, performing regular audits and assessments to ensure alignment with evolving business dynamics and regulatory requirements. These evaluations help maintain compliance, enhance operational efficiency, and adapt to industry developments as needed.
Compliance with statutory requirements of relevance to the principles, and, rectification of any non-compliance- The Company ensures prompt compliance with all statutory requirements by conducting annual reviews to stay informed of regulatory updates and maintain adherence to evolving mandates.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: Corporate Governance and Sustainability, Operational Training – Hard and Soft Skills, Human Rights on various parameters conducted during Induction
Corporate Governance and Business Ethics, Operational Training – Hard and Soft Skills, Human Rights on various parameters conducted during Induction

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol: The Company has incorporated Anti-Corruption and Anti-Bribery provisions into its Code of Conduct and Ethical Business Practice Policy which is available on the Company’s Intranet. This clearly forbids any participation in bribery, the offering or receiving of illegal payments, or the acceptance of bribes by the company, its employees, or any intermediaries. It also applies to any attempts to secure or maintain business through such practices. Acknowledging the potential legal risks under local anti-corruption laws due to engagements with third parties, the company stresses the importance of compliance with these laws through its Code of Conduct.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: During the reporting period, the Company did not face any instances of conflict of interest, corruption, or receive any fines, penalties, or actions from regulators, law enforcement, or judicial bodies.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheEntityHaveProcesse:
Yes, The Company’s Code of Conduct outlines clear expectations regarding conflict of interest for its Directors and Senior Management. They are required to avoid any situation where their personal interests may conflict, or appear to conflict, with those of the Company. The members of the Boards must uphold professionalism, and act in ethical and honest manner. Any actual or potential conflicts must be promptly disclosed to the appropriate authority. Furthermore, Directors and Senior Management must not engage in any transaction or activity-directly or indirectly, that could compromise their ability to act solely in the best interests of the Company.
Web Link of the same can be accessed at: https://aether.co.in/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Code-of-Conduct-for-the-Company.pdf.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple1ExplanatoryText:
Essential 2- Directors/ KMPs have not been subjected to any thresholds of the materiality policy to pay any fines, penalties, punishments, awards, compounding fees, or settlement amounts in the financial year.
Essential 6- The Company’s Code of Conduct outlines clear expectations regarding conflict of interest for its Directors and Senior Management. They are required to avoid any situation where their personal interests may conflict, or appear to conflict, with those of the Company. Any actual or potential conflicts must be promptly disclosed to the appropriate authority. Furthermore, Directors and Senior Management must not engage in any transaction or activity-directly or indirectly, that could compromise their ability to act solely in the best interests of the Company.
Leadership 1- The Company actively engages with its value chain partners through a variety of channels, including emails, site visits, group meetings, one-on-one interactions, phone calls, SMS, exhibitions, and gatherings. These interactions help build strong relationships and enable the Company to better understand and respond to the needs of its partners. Although formal awareness programs are not yet established, the Company intends to evaluate their implementation for its value-chain partners.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfImprovementsInEnviron: To ensure sustainable power for our operations, we have built a 16 MW solar power generation plant in Sarod Village, Bharuch District, Gujarat, which supplies electricity to our three manufacturing facilities that became operational in Fiscal year 2023. This solar power plant will cover the daytime electricity needs of these facilities. Moreover, we have installed a Solvent Recovery Plant (SRP) to minimize waste generation. We have partnered with government-affiliated agencies for the disposal of liquid and powder chemical waste after treatment on our end. Furthermore, an order for a 15 MW hybrid power plant has also been awarded.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesInPlaceToS: As a company in Chemical Sector, we recognize the importance of minimizing waste and reducing our environmental footprint. To achieve this, we have implemented a comprehensive waste recycling program focused on reducing waste generation and identifying innovative reuse and recycling solutions. Our program includes key initiatives: a. Waste Reduction: Implementing efficient production methods, optimizing raw material use, and improving supply chain management to minimize waste. b. Waste Segregation: Ensuring recyclable materials are properly separated to maximize reuse and recycling potential. c. Recycling Partnerships: Collaborating with local recycling facilities and operating our Solvent Recovery Plant to enhance waste recycling efforts. d. Education and Training: Equipping employees with the knowledge and skills to actively participate in waste reduction and recycling initiatives. e. Innovation: Investing in research and development to explore new technologies for repurposing waste into valuable products. Our waste recycling efforts have not only reduced our environmental impact but have also created business opportunities and cost efficiencies. By adopting a circular economy approach, we conserve resources, minimize waste, and generate value for our customers and stakeholders. We consider our waste recycling program a fundamental part of our sustainability commitment and responsible business practices. Our dedication to exploring new and innovative ways to reduce our environmental impact remains unwavering as we strive toward a more sustainable future.

Essential 1- The Company consistently assesses its R&D expenditures, which are allocated specifically for the company’s R&D activities and do not significantly contribute to environmental or social factors. However, by incorporating social and environmental factors, the company shall focus on its dedication to sustainable practices and responsible decision-making that aligns with its long-term business goals.
Essential 2 a- Yes, the company is committed to integrating social, ethical, and environmental factors across its supply chain.
Essential 2 b- The Company sourced approximately 15% of its inputs sustainably, including the organic chemicals used in operations. It consistently ensures that all inputs are procured through sustainable means.
Essential 4- All products and packaging waste, including plastics, e-waste, hazardous, residuary, and battery waste, are responsibly reclaimed and handed over to authorized vendors for reuse, recycling, or safe disposal, though exact quantities are yet to be quantified.
Essential 5- The Company ensures responsible reclamation of products and packaging waste through authorized vendors for reuse, recycling, or safe disposal. The exact quantities are yet to be quantified.
Essential 8 - The above calculations are in accordance with Part B, Attribute 9 of the Industry Standards Note (December 2024) on the Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR) Core, jointly issued by ASSOCHAM, CII, and FICCI, pursuant to Regulation 34(2) of the SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, and as per SEBI Circular No. SEBI/HO/CFD/CFD-SEC-2/P/CIR/2023/122.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfEqualOppertunityPolic: The Company takes pride in being an equal opportunity employer that values diversity and adaptability within its workforce. These principles are embedded in its Code of Conduct, which is accessible on the intranet. Committed to fostering a safe and supportive workplace, the Company believes that such an environment enables employees to perform at their best. It is dedicated to cultivating an inclusive culture where every individual feels respected, valued, and empowered. To support its employees, the Company has introduced various initiatives, including regular training sessions, wellness programs, and a culture that encourages open communication and collaboration. The Company strives to create a workplace culture that celebrates diversity and adaptability, ensuring equal opportunities for all employees to succeed.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR:
Yes, the Company has established a comprehensive grievance resolution procedure for its employees. Employees can initially raise their concerns with their immediate manager. If the issue remains unresolved, the respective HR serves as the next point of contact, responsible for investigating the matter and ensuring a fair and transparent resolution.
For POSH (Prevention of Sexual Harassment) matters, employees are expected to report concerns to the local HR team or via the dedicated email ID for the Internal Complaints (IC) Committee. If necessary, the matter can be escalated to the Chairperson of the IC Committee, with the final level of escalation being the Head HR of the company.
The Company is committed to ensuring that employee’s feel heard and supported in addressing their grievances. The grievance redressal procedure is a key element of its commitment to fostering a positive and respectful workplace culture.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: Yes, the Company regularly conducts fire drills and safety exercises in full compliance with legal requirements. It also undergoes periodic third-party audits to evaluate the effectiveness of its safety systems. The Company has obtained ISO 45001:2018 - Occupational Health & Safety Management System for GIDC Plot No. 8203 and Hojiwala. It remains committed to promoting the physical health and mental well-being of its employees.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo:
Employees are encouraged to report any work-related safety concerns to their HR Managers, and the Company takes prompt action to address or enhance safety measures as needed. Regular internal communications and notifications are shared to promote precautionary measures against safety risks. Moreover, safety awareness sessions are conducted, and all office staff receive continuous training in both basic and advanced fire safety, including participation in escape drills.
Process for Safety Considerations:
a. Hazard Identification: Comprehensive hazard assessments are conducted to identify potential risks such as chemical releases, fires, explosions, and equipment failures. b. Risk Management: Process hazard analyses (PHAs) and other risk management strategies are implemented to mitigate process-related hazards. c. Emergency Preparedness: Emergency response plans are developed and maintained, including employee training, drills, simulations, and coordination with external emergency response agencies. d. Asset Integrity: Regular maintenance and inspections of process equipment are prioritized to ensure reliability and minimize the risk of failures and associated hazards. e. Management of Change: Established procedures ensure that any changes to processes are thoroughly assessed for safety implications before implementation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE:
At the Company, the health, safety, and well-being of employees remain a top priority. It continuously reviews and updates its systems, policies, and processes to adapt to the evolving work environment. It strictly complies with all applicable laws and regulations, implementing precautionary and preventive measures across all operating locations.
The company has an SOP that establishes the procedure for providing a timely and high-quality AE Cafe facility. This procedure covers the hygiene and management of the cafe. The AE Cafe Committee is responsible for managing the cafe, including maintaining the menu, ensuring safe working conditions, and checking the quality of raw materials.
To promote physical and mental well-being, the Company offers a range of employee wellness initiatives, including annual health check-ups, health risk assessments, advisory services, medical insurance, personal accident and life coverage, an employee assistance helpline, and access to doctors, emergency services, and ambulance services. Regular employee feedback is sought to assess various aspects of physical and mental well-being, helping to identify gaps and develop new programs.
The Company is committed to providing employees with the necessary resources and support to maintain their health, safety, and productivity, both professionally and personally.
Employee Benefits provided include: a. Health and accident insurance b. Mediclaim insurance (family floater cover and corporate buffer) with an option for top-up c. Parental leave, including maternity and adoption leave d. Retirement benefits such as Provident Fund (PF), Gratuity, and Employee State Insurance (ESI) e. Earned (Privilege) Leave and Casual/Sick Leave
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: The Company promptly addresses all safety-related risks identified through internal assessments and employee feedback. During the year 2024–25, no safety incidents were reported, nor were any employee grievances related to safety received. Multiple safety measures are implemented on a day-to-day basis to ensure a secure working environment. In addition, an external agency has been engaged to provide expert guidance, and their recommendations and best practices are regularly incorporated. As these initiatives are ongoing in nature and form part of continuous improvement.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple3ExplanatoryText:

Note: Other than permanent workers are covered under statutory / non-statutory insurances and other protections are being taken care by their Contractor / Principal.
Essential 1 c. - The above calculations are in accordance with Part B, Attribute 5 of the Industry Standards Note (December 2024) on the Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR) Core, jointly issued by ASSOCHAM, CII, and FICCI, pursuant to Regulation 34(2) of the SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, and as per SEBI Circular No. SEBI/HO/CFD/CFD-SEC-2/P/CIR/2023/122.
Essential 3 - Yes, The Company offices are equipped with accessibility features for differently-abled employees in compliance with the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016. Facilities such as lifts, illuminated corridors, automated taps, and other similar features ensure ease of access for differently-abled employees and workers.
Essential 7 - The Company recognizes the right to freedom of association and has an internal procedure outlining the employees’ freedom to join associations. This affirms the right of all employees to freedom of association and collective bargaining without discrimination. However, none of the Company’s employees or workers are affiliated with any associations or unions. The Company does not impose any restrictions on the formation of such associations or unions.
Essential 9 - The Company has 360° Employee Evaluation Policy that ensures a fair and transparent evaluation of employees by assessing key performance areas, achievements, and contributions. Appraisees provide their self-assessment, while appraisers record remarks, percentage achievements, and approval ratings. The process promotes accountability, recognition, and alignment of individual goals with organizational objectives.
Essential 10c - Yes, the Company has established processes for workers to report work-related hazards and to remove themselves from such risks, as outlined in its safety mitigation strategies.
A. Workplace Safety and Risk Mitigation The Company recognizes that workplace safety risks, particularly those related to utilities, fire hazards, and construction, are inherent. These risks are continuously identified, and necessary corrective measures are implemented. Employees are encouraged to report any safety-related concerns to their Health and Safety Managers for prompt resolution.
B. Mitigation Strategies
i. Engineering Controls: a. Physical barriers and guards are installed on machinery to prevent contact with moving parts and minimize the risk of injuries. b. Automated equipment is utilized where possible to reduce manual handling and ergonomic strain. c. Ventilation systems are implemented to control exposure to airborne contaminants and maintain air quality in enclosed spaces.
ii. Administrative Controls: a. Standard operating procedures (SOPs) are developed and communicated to ensure safe and consistent task execution. b. Regular training programs educate employees on hazard recognition, safe work practices, and emergency procedures. c. Job rotation and ergonomic assessments are introduced to mitigate the risk of musculoskeletal disorders caused by repetitive tasks. d. Reporting systems encourage employees to promptly report hazards, near misses, and incidents for investigation and corrective action.
iii. Personal Protective Equipment (PPE): a. Employees are provided with appropriate PPE, including safety glasses, gloves, respiratory protection, and hearing protection, based on workplace hazards. b. Training is conducted on the correct use, maintenance, and storage of PPE to ensure its effectiveness in safeguarding employees from occupational risks.
Essential 10d - Yes, the Company provides comprehensive benefits, resources, and crisis assistance to employees and their families, including medical expense support through: a. An employee assistance helpline for guidance and support. b. Medi-claim insurance, which includes family floater coverage and a corporate buffer.
Essential 14 - Note: All company plants and offices have been assessed by an external agency for health, safety, and working conditions.
Leadership 1- Yes, the Company provides Group Life and Group Personal Accident Insurance to all employees and on-roll workers, along with Provident Fund and Gratuity Benefits. In the event of an employee’s or worker’s death, the Company ensures that the proceeds from these benefits are extended to the designated family member or nominee.
Leadership 4- The Company currently does not have formal transition assistance programs to support continued employability or manage career endings arising from retirement or termination.
Leadership 5- The Company actively collaborates with its value chain partners by conducting assessments through a questionnaire distributed via email, collecting information based on their responses. Approximately 2% of the partners have been assessed on these parameters.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMeasuresUndertakenByT: The Company has implemented several measures to ensure that statutory dues are correctly deducted and deposited by its value chain partners. A regular payment cycle is maintained, with close monitoring and verification to ensure compliance with legal requirements. Moreover, the Company prioritizes payments to Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSME) vendors, acknowledging their significance in the value chain and facilitating timely payments.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif:
The Company’s business operations are closely connected to the social and environment factors, impacting various stakeholder groups, including employees, customers, investors, and the community. To create value for all stakeholders, the Company prioritizes trust-building and actively engages with them through multiple channels to drive sustainability initiatives and achieve economic and environment sustainability goals.
The Company conducts comprehensive stakeholder analysis to assess and rank risks based on their impact on business operations and influence on the Company. It maintains strong relationships with investors who contribute to its capital and support its commitment to value creation. A customer-centric approach determines the Company’s dedication to serving its customers, while sustained value creation reflects its commitment to shareholders. The Company moreover collaborates with industry experts, regulatory bodies, and academic institutions to stay informed about evolving market trends.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideTheProcessesForConsulta: The Company engages with various stakeholder groups through multiple channels throughout the year to ensure effective two-way communication. Employee-level committees report stakeholder feedback to the ESG Committee at the management level. Oversight of stakeholder engagement on key economic, environmental, and social matters is provided by the Board Committee on CSR & Sustainability, which ensures the effective implementation of consultation channels.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesAsToHowTheIn: Yes, the company, guided by its core principles, has established channels for active engagement with all stakeholder groups, enhancing its competitive edge and building brand trust. Regular interactions with key stakeholders ensure that business operations align with their interests in a sustainable and profitable manner. Based on discussions with the investment community, the Company has aligned its environmental management goals with the global transition to a low-carbon economy, updating its policies and internal systems to reinforce its commitment as a responsible business brand.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesOfEngagement:
The Company has established an active engagement channel with the community to address any grievances related to its operations and community development programs. In the fiscal year 2024-25, no grievances were reported by stakeholder groups, and any concerns raised were promptly addressed through the Company’s grievance redressal mechanism.
The Company’s Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives are focused on creating sustainable solutions that benefit both the community and the environment. The Company is committed to actively contributing to societal development and environmental preservation within its operational sphere, fostering long-term growth as a socially responsible entity.
Through its CSR efforts, the Company strives to make a meaningful impact on people’s lives and promote a healthier, more inclusive society. These initiatives encompass various activities, including promoting education, supporting elderly care facilities, providing training for nationally recognized and Olympic sports, offering healthcare services, reducing inequalities among disadvantaged groups, implementing life-saving measures, and extending medical aid and support.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple4ExplanatoryText: Essential 2 - Customer are identified as marhinalised/vulnerable based on specified criteria such as income, gender, etc. However, the Company does not specifically identify any marginalized segment as its customer base.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI:
The Company has established multiple policies and platforms to address employee grievances related to human rights, ensuring a fair and transparent resolution process. These include:
a. Whistle Blower Policy: The Organization has a Whistle Blower policy that allows employees to raise concerns or grievances anonymously while ensuring confidentiality. A dedicated email ID is available at the organizational level for employees to express their concerns, and a designated SPOC is responsible for handling all matters independently and fairly. b. Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH) Guidelines: To provide a safe and equitable work environment, the Organization has implemented POSH guidelines. Awareness programs are conducted periodically to ensure employees are informed about their rights. Employees can report harassment concerns to the IC Committee via a dedicated email ID or approach the HR team alternatively. Each reported case is investigated by an IC Committee in accordance with the POSH Act, and necessary actions are taken. c. Fair Appeal: This platform enables employees to raise concerns regarding the fairness of the performance management process. Employees can appeal against unjust performance evaluations by submitting relevant facts and evidence. All grievances are thoroughly reviewed to ensure fair and transparent resolutions. d. Appellate Authority: If an employee is dissatisfied with the outcome of an investigation or the resolution provided, they have the option to appeal to the Company’s Appellate Authority for further review.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons: Employees can raise grievances through designated channels, including the Chairman, the Audit Committee, or the Chief Compliance Officer. If the resolution provided by the local HR team is unsatisfactory, concerns can be escalated to the regional or national HR team. The Company has also implemented a Whistle Blower Policy to uphold transparency and accountability. To protect complainants from any adverse consequences, the Company ensures the highest level of confidentiality regarding their identity. All individuals involved in a reported complaint are made aware of the strict prohibition against retaliatory actions. If any instance of retaliation is observed or reported, the Company promptly takes appropriate action to address the matter and ensures that the complainant does not face any negative repercussions. Moreover, matters related to discrimination and its impact are addressed by the POSH Committee.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfABusinessProcessBeing: As there have been no human rights grievances or complaints, no specific business process has been modified. The Employees can raise grievances through designated channels, including the Chairman, the Audit Committee, or the Chief Compliance Officer. If the resolution provided by the local HR team is unsatisfactory, concerns can be escalated to the regional or national HR team. The Company has implemented a Whistle Blower Policy to uphold transparency and accountability. This policy enables employees to report concerns without fear of retaliation, discrimination, or disadvantage. Moreover, matters related to discrimination and its impact are addressed by the POSH Committee.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheScopeAndCoverageOf: The Company ensures compliance with all applicable regulations through periodic audits and a structured due diligence conducted by an External Agency . Regular internal audits and evaluations are conducted to assess the effectiveness of these policies and procedures related to human rights.

Essential 10- Note: All company plants and offices have been assessed by an external agency for the above parameters relating to Human Rights.
The Company has an internal procedure that specifies a policy on human rights and human trafficking. This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and business associates and prohibits the use of forced, compulsory, or child labor. It also ensures fair wages, working hours, and a workplace free from discrimination and harassment. It also has a procedure that specifies a “No Child Labour Policy.” This policy prohibits the employment of any person under the age of eighteen and ensures that no one is made to work against their will.
Leadership 3 - The offices fully adhere to the guidelines outlined in the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016. They are equipped with accessibility features such as lifts, automated taps, and illuminated corridors to ensure ease of access for differently-abled visitors.
Leadership 4 - The Company actively collaborates with its value chain partners by conducting assessments through a questionnaire distributed via email, collecting information based on their responses. Approximately 2% of the partners have been assessed on these parameters.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfCoverageAndImplementa:
The entity has successfully implemented a Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) system designed to treat wastewater and significantly minimize liquid waste generation. The treatment process is outlined below:
Effluent Treatment Process
Industrial effluent generated from various operational processes is collected and treated in an Effluent Treatment Plant (ETP) equipped with a primary treatment facility:
Collection & Neutralization
Effluent is first gathered in a collection tank, then transferred to an equalization cum neutralization tank, where it is held for a sufficient duration. The pH level is raised to approximately 8.5 by dosing lime solution from a dedicated lime dosing tank.
Primary Treatment
The neutralized effluent is pumped into a primary settling tank, allowing suspended solids to settle. The clarified effluent is collected in a secondary collection tank, while the settled sludge is dewatered using a filter press and the dried sludge is sent to a TSDF site for secure landfilling. The filtrate is recirculated back to the neutralization tank for further treatment.
Advanced Treatment
The primary treated effluent is directed to a stripper unit to remove low volatile organics. The residual bottom material is processed through a Multiple Effect Evaporator (MEE) or Mechanical Vapor Recompression (MVR) system. The condensate from the evaporation process is sent to the Secondary Biological Treatment (SBT) plant for additional treatment.
Secondary Biological Treatment (SBT)
The SBT process is a nature-based biological treatment that utilizes respiration, mineral weathering, and photosynthesis within a controlled environment. It employs selected micro- and macro-organisms to enhance the purification process through the following steps:
Biological Conversion
a. Respiration oxidizes organic molecules to remove contaminants. b. Mineral weathering helps in adjusting the pH. c. Photosynthesis regulates overall system activity.
Pre-Treatment & Neutralization
Incoming wastewater, with a pH in the range of 6–7, is treated with lime to adjust the pH before filtration. Advanced Oxidation An ozone generator is used to break down complex molecules, enhancing the Biological Oxygen Demand (BOD)/Chemical Oxygen Demand (COD) ratio.
Batch Biological Treatment a. Wastewater is pumped to the top of the bioreactor, which contains 40–60 mm media embedded with cultured microorganisms. b. The water percolates through the media and is collected in a bottom tank. c. For optimal solid-liquid contact, the water may be recirculated through the media. d. Manual removal provisions are provided for suspended solids from the bio-filter surface.
Final Purification
a. Dissolved organic and inorganic materials are oxidized, and the purified effluent is fed into a Reverse Osmosis (RO) Plant. b. RO Treatment and Water Reuse c. The RO permeate (clean water) is recycled for use in plant utilities. d. The RO reject is further subjected to evaporation, and the resulting condensate is also recycled into the utilities loop.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProjectRelatedToReduc:
The Company is steadily advancing its journey toward adopting sustainable business practices with a focus on reducing its environmental footprint. As part of this transition, the Company has integrated the ConvergeO` platform - a cutting-edge, innovative technology aimed at manufacturing more sustainable polyols. These differentiated series of polyols can incorporate up to 40% carbon dioxide by weight, significantly reducing the overall CO2 emissions. Designed specifically for the CASE industry (Coatings, Adhesives, Sealants, and Elastomers), this series of polyols is differentiated by its sustainability credentials and performance potential.
The commercialization of these polyols presents a significant revenue opportunity, targeting a market size of approximately 850 KTA (850,000 metric tonnes per annum), with an anticipated CAGR of 5%. This represents a substantial portion of the broader CASE industry polyol market, which exceeds 10,000 KTA. Through this strategic adoption, the Company reinforces its commitment to low-carbon innovation and sustainable product development, contributing meaningfully to environmental responsibility and long-term business growth.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti:
The Company has implemented a range of strong waste management practices that reflect its commitment to minimizing environmental impact, conserving natural resources, and promoting sustainability across operations. By integrating these best practices, the Company continues to make significant strides in reducing its environment footprint and enhancing operational efficiency.
Key waste and resource management initiatives include:
a. Installation of a 100 KL Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) Plant to ensure efficient effluent treatment and complete reuse of treated water. b. Utilization of renewable energy sources, including equity-based and power purchase agreement (PPA)-based solar energy, meeting 100% of the energy needs across all operational units. c. Adoption of biodiesel as an alternative fuel to reduce dependence on conventional fossil fuels. d. Deployment of a 100 TR Brine Chiller and a 75 HP Cooling Tower to optimize cooling efficiency and conserve energy. e. Cessation of groundwater usage, demonstrating a responsible approach to water resource conservation. f. No direct air emissions, contributing to cleaner air and compliance with environmental standards. g. In-house Solvent Recovery Plant (SRP) for recovering and reusing solvents within the process, enhancing resource efficiency and reducing chemical waste. h. Development of a green space (‘Aether Van’) within the industrial premises, promoting biodiversity and improving site aesthetics. i. Utilization of a community Effluent Treatment Plant (ETP) for shared wastewater management solutions. j. Use of a community boiler to meet steam requirements instead of installing a dedicated in-house boiler, reducing emissions and resource consumption.
Through these initiatives, the Company reinforces its dedication to responsible environmental conservation and continuous improvement in waste and resource management practices.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSignificantDirectAndI: The Company refrains from conducting its operations in environmentally fragile or ecologically sensitive regions. This strategic decision underscores the company’s commitment to responsible business practices and environmental stewardship, avoiding potential harm to delicate ecosystems. By deliberately choosing locations that are not ecologically sensitive, the company aims to minimize its environmental impact and contribute to the preservation of biodiversity and natural habitats.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DisclosureWebLinkOfEntityAtWhi: Yes, the Company has instituted a strong Business Continuity Management (BCM) policy, supported by a well-defined BCM plan to effectively manage and minimize the impact of unexpected disruptions. This Board-approved policy provides a structured framework for the recovery and restoration of critical information, continuity of operations, and timely response to incidents arising from natural calamities, technical failures, human error, or pandemics. Recognizing that disruptions can occur unpredictably, the company’s BCM plan is designed to proactively address operational risks such as business interruptions and system failures, ensuring resilience and uninterrupted service delivery.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DiscloseAnySignificantAdverseI: Given the nature of its operations, the Company has not recorded any negative impacts on environmental resources resulting from its business activities. The Company remains committed to responsible environmental conservation and continuously monitors its operations to ensure alignment with sustainability goals and regulatory requirements.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo:
The Company has established a structured escalation mechanism to address customer complaints, which is clearly outlined on the Company’s website. Customers can access a three-tier grievance redressal process:
a. Customers may raise their concerns through accessible channels such as email, toll-free numbers, or written communication. b. In case the resolution provided is unsatisfactory, the grievance can be escalated to the Deputy Vice President - Customer Support. c. If the issue remains unresolved, the customer may further escalate the concern to the Head of Customer Support.
It has adopted the ISO 9001:2015 - Quality Management System (QMS) to ensure consistent quality in products and services, customer satisfaction, continuous improvement, and process efficiency. At GIDC Plot No. 8202, 8203, and Hojiwala.
The Company’s continuous focus on process efficiency, innovation, and digital integration has contributed to improved customer satisfaction and experience.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfThePolicyOnCyberSecur:
Yes, the Company has established a structured and comprehensive information and cyber security framework that ensures data protection and privacy through strong management systems across the organisation. The Company has adopted an Information and Cyber Security (ICS) Policy approved by the Board, which is aligned with the IRDAI cyber security requirements and international ISO standards.
The cyber security controls and cloud computing practices conform to ISO 27001:2013 - the globally recognised standard for Information Security Management Systems. This enables the Company to proactively detect, mitigate, and respond to threats across its networks, infrastructure, and applications.
To manage cyber risks, the Company implements a combination of technological safeguards and procedural controls, following leading cyber security guidelines and IRDAI. The Company adopts a risk-based approach to evaluate and deploy suitable cyber security technologies. The Company evaluates and implements various security technologies and solutions to help address cyber risks through risk based approach. The administration, application and efficacy of the cyber security policy are all monitored by a team of independent internal auditors.
In addition, a competent third-party performs an independent assurance audit each year to assess if these policies are adequate in light of regulatory bodies requirement. The policy is on the Company’s intranet and is easily accessible to all internal stakeholders.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT:
The Company, as a responsible and ethical, has established strong systems and processes to ensure adherence to fair trade practices across its operations. The Company has a strong and continually upgraded IT infrastructure that supports the highest standards of data security and system integrity.
During the reporting period, the Company did not receive any complaints related to advertising, provision of essential services, cybersecurity breaches, or customer data privacy issues. Moreover, there were no instances of product recalls, nor were there any penalties or regulatory actions imposed concerning the safety of its goods or services.

Customers are also guided on adopting specific risk-mitigation measures.
